content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
March 10, 1986 
Mary L. Faust, Treasurer 
Alliance for Responsible Government 
25127 Elder Avenue 
Moreno Valley, CA 
92388 
Re: 
FPPC Advice No. A-86-046 
Dear Ms. Faust: 
This is in response to your recent letter requesting advice 
regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political 
Reform Act (Government Code Sections 81000-91015). 
Specifically, 
you asked whether a $500 contribution received by the Alliance 
for Responsible Government on January 31, 1986, must be disclosed 
in a late contribution report. 
During our telephone conversation 
on or about February 6, 1986, you stated that the Alliance for 
Responsible Government is primarily formed to support or oppose 
candidates or measures involved in a March 4, 1986 city election. 
as: 
Government Code Section 82036 defines "late contribution" 
... any contribution including a loan which totals in 
the aggregate one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more 
that is made to or received by a candidate, a controlled 
committee, or a committee formed or existing primarily 
to support or oppose a candidate or measure before the 
date of the election at which the candidate or measure 
is to be voted on but after the closing date of the last 
campaign statement required to be filed prior to the 
election by a candidate or committee participating in 
such election. 
The late contribution reporting period for the March 4 
election was February 16 through March 3. 
Therefore, only 
contributions totaling $1,000 or more, in the aggregate, from 
a single source received during that period were required to be 
reported as late contributions. 
Since the $500 contribution received on January 31 does not 
meet the definition of a "late contribution," no late contribution 
report was required to be filed with respect to that contribution. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 


Mary L. Faust 
March 10, 1986 
Page 2 
I hope this information is helpful. 
Please let me know if 
I can be of further assistance. 
Sincerely, 
Carla Wardlow 
Political Reform Consultant 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
February 10, 1986 
Mary L. Faust, Treasurer 
Alliance for Responsible Government 
25127 Elder Avenue 
Moreno Valley, CA 
92388 
Re: 
86-046 
Dear Ms. Faust: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act has been received by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice 
request, you may contact the Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or unless more information is needed to answer your request, 
you should expect a response within 21 working days. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, 
9~~ 
Jeanne Pritchard 
Chief 
Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800. P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question: Whether a $500 contribution received by the Alliance for Responsible Government on January 31, 1986, must be disclosed in a late contribution report.
sections.conclusion: Since the $500 contribution received on January 31 does not meet the definition of a "late contribution," no late contribution report was required to be filed with respect to that contribution.
sections.question_synthetic: Must a $500 contribution received on January 31, 1986 be reported as a late contribution for a March 4, 1986 city election?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The $500 contribution does not qualify as a late contribution because late contributions must total $1,000 or more in the aggregate from a single source and be received during the late contribution reporting period (February 16 through March 3). Since the contribution was received on January 31 and was only $500, no late contribution report was required.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of two pages: an FPPC acknowledgment letter dated February 10, 1986, and the substantive advice letter dated March 10, 1986. Both addressed to Mary L. Faust regarding Advice No. A-86-046. Handwritten notations on final page were not analyzed.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a $500 contribution received by the Alliance for Responsible Government on January 31, 1986, must be disclosed in a late contribution report.

CONCLUSION: Since the $500 contribution received on January 31 does not meet the definition of a "late contribution," no late contribution report was required to be filed with respect to that contribution.

embedding.summary: FPPC advised Alliance for Responsible Government that a $500 contribution received on January 31, 1986 does not meet the definition of a late contribution under Government Code Section 82036 and therefore does not require disclosure in a late contribution report for the March 4, 1986 election.
